📖 EQ Legends — Ultimate Paladin / Monk / Shaman Guide

Difficulty 4 · Level 1–50 · Farming · Loot · Raids
PaladinMonkShaman
⚠️ READ FIRST — Legends systems🧭 Start⚡ THE PATH★ Must-have🎯 Entry lvl1 Intro2 Leveling🗺️ Travel🔁 Farm Loops3 Atlas4 Named DB 5 Loot DB6 Trio BiS 7 Strategy8 Money9 Raids10 Endgame ★ Bonus 🗺️ Maps ▸1 Befallen2 Najena3 Lower Guk4 Crushbone5 Splitpaw· Unrest:① Main② Ghoul Magus③ Undead Knight④ Garanel⑤ Lesser

Starting zones

Two races, two starts — both funnel into THE PATH by Befallen (level 10-15).

Human — Freeport. Level in the Commonlands newbie yards to about 5, then push into the West Commonlands. Befallen is right there, so you join THE PATH immediately, and neutral faction lets you walk into any city to shop.

Froglok — Lake Rathe. Clear the newbie critters around the froglok camp to about level 5, then drift into Innothule Swamp and the Feerrott. You start friendly with the living Guk frogloks, so you are born right next to your best mid-game zone. Pick up THE PATH at Befallen (10-15).

⚡ THE PATH — level by level

The one table to scan: what level → where to go → what to grab. ★ = don't-miss BiS. Full routes/locs/maps for each zone are in the chapters below.
LvlGo toFarm / grab
1–5Commonlands (Freeport)rats, snakes, decaying skeletons outside Freeport → reach 5
5–12West Commonlands (Orc Highway)orcs and skeletons — steady XP; Befallen's entrance is right here
10–15Befallen (W. Commonlands) 🗺★ Pristine Leather Set + Enchanted Fine Steel Mace · Barbed plate · undead = your Paladin
15–22Najena (Lavastorm) 🗺★ Flaming Fist ×2 (Unbound Flame) · ★ Kitchen Toolbelt haste (The Tenderizer ~20) · Clawed Knuckle Ring
20–30Estate of Unrest (Faydwer) 🗺climb floors → Bloodstained Tunic (28) · Dwarven Work Boots (35) · Small Bronze set (merge). Alt: Runnyeye / Sol A
28–33Upper Guk (home turf) 🗺★ Ghoulbane — Froglok Shin Lord, Barracks (-5,288). As a Human you're KOS to the living frogloks — pull to the zone line or work the dead side
30–40Lower Guk — dead side 🗺★ FBSS (Frenzied Ghoul) · ★ Sword of Ykesha (Ghoul Lord ~37 (1558,-749))
40–45The Hole (Odus)★ Idol of the Underking
45–50Planes (46+) / Sebilis / Karnorendgame gear + resists → 50 (race unlock token)
Human note: you start neutral, so you can shop in any city. From Freeport the Commonlands → Befallen lane carries you to your first real gear — the Lower Guk dead side (undead) is hostile to everyone, so that farm (FBSS, Ykesha) needs no faction.
LvlGo toFarm / grab
1–5Rathe Mountains (start)newbie mobs around the froglok camp → reach 5
5–12Feerrott / Innothule Swampsnakes, spiders, undead — steady XP; you're right on Guk's doorstep
10–15Befallen (W. Commonlands) 🗺★ Pristine Leather Set + Enchanted Fine Steel Mace · Barbed plate · undead = your Paladin
15–22Najena (Lavastorm) 🗺★ Flaming Fist ×2 (Unbound Flame) · ★ Kitchen Toolbelt haste (The Tenderizer ~20) · Clawed Knuckle Ring
20–30Estate of Unrest (Faydwer) 🗺climb floors → Bloodstained Tunic (28) · Dwarven Work Boots (35) · Small Bronze set (merge). Alt: Runnyeye / Sol A
28–33Upper Guk (home turf) 🗺★ Ghoulbane — Froglok Shin Lord, Barracks (-5,288). Froglok faction = you move easier vs the living Guk
30–40Lower Guk — dead side 🗺★ FBSS (Frenzied Ghoul) · ★ Sword of Ykesha (Ghoul Lord ~37 (1558,-749))
40–45The Hole (Odus)★ Idol of the Underking
45–50Planes (46+) / Sebilis / Karnorendgame gear + resists → 50 (race unlock token)
Froglok note: you start friendly with the living Guk frogloks, so Upper Guk & Lower Guk live-side are easier for you — the dead side (undead) is your gear farm (FBSS, Ykesha). Being a Froglok = you're born next to your best mid-game zone.

★ Items you must NOT miss (10 → 40)

Low-level start (you're at Blackburrow, lvl 10): Blackburrow has no keeper gear — it's XP + Gnoll Fangs (turn in to Captain Tillin, Qeynos Arena balcony). If the entrance gnolls con green / XP is crawling, you've out-levelled them → raise difficulty to D1-D2, or just leave for Befallen now (better XP + your first real items: Pristine Leather + Enchanted Fine Steel Mace). Travel routes = the 🗺️ Travel section.

🎯 At what level can you start each dungeon?

"Start" = you can safely farm the entrance/first area. "BiS reachable" = the level of the named that drop your key gear (with Slow + tank you can pull a few levels above your own). Undead zones are easier for you (Paladin).
DungeonStart ~lvlBiS named reachableNote
Blackburrow4–5— (XP + fangs only)entrance ring 7-9; you're here now
Befallen6–8skeletons (Pristine) ~8-14 → Thaumaturgist 18 → Baron Telyx 28undead → Paladin. Your first real gear
Crushbone6–88–18 (belt/bracers merge set)Faydwer (boat from Freeport) — optional
Najena15 (front rooms)The Tenderizer 22 · Unbound Flame 25 · Drelzna 25 · Najena 29★ Kitchen Toolbelt/haste ~20-22, Flaming Fist ~25
Estate of Unrest12–14 (yard)Blade Fiend 19 → Ghoul Magus 23 → Barkeep 25 → Undead Knight 28 → Garanel 35climb the manor as you level
Runnyeye2020–30underrated, low competition
Sol A (Solusek's Eye)2020–30XP loop
Upper Guk5–25 (deep camps 25-33)Ghoulbane ~28-33 (Shin Lord · Barracks 2nd layer · (-5,288) · rare)stage for Lower Guk
Lower Guk28–3030–40 (Ghoul Lord, Frenzied Ghoul, Cavalier ~37)dead side = undead; bring an evac
The Hole4040+ (Idol of the Underking)deep, evac needed
Fear / Hate / Sky46+raid gearkey/flag + group
Your path right now (lvl 10): Blackburrow → Befallen at ~10 (first floor, con it) → Najena at 15 for haste → Unrest yard at 12-14 and climb it → Upper Guk for Ghoulbane ~30 → Lower Guk 30+ (Ykesha ~37).
🛠️ Latest patch — Jul 14, 2026: weapon procs now fire properly — Ghoulbane and Ykesha finally do what this guide describes · every Mote of Potential is now No Trade: farm them, you can no longer buy or trade one · NPCs with a secondary melee class now use bash, kick, cleave, frenzy and backstab — more stuns coming at you, which is exactly what Steadfast Will is for (Chapter 11) · OverChannel resist interactions fixed, so your Slow and DoTs land more often · the Estate of Unrest now keeps more of its doors locked — expect to need the key chain, and check the no-key route to the Hand yourself before trusting it · Crushbone rebuilt (levels 4-22) and Lair of the Splitpaw rebuilt (25-42), both with far higher rare-spawn and drop rates · voidlings now see invisible players inside instances. The Jul 7 changes are already folded into the chapters above. Live launch 28 Jul — official patch notes.

Chapter 1 — Introduction

Why Paladin / Monk / Shaman is S-Tier

A complete self-sufficient unit: Shaman Slow defangs every melee mob, Paladin tanks + controls + wrecks undead, Monk deletes the slowed target. You don't need a group — you need patience. The trio dominates long, controlled fights.

Strengths
  • ★★★★★ vs single bosses (Slow + sustain + steady DPS)
  • Anti-undead (Paladin) — huge in Befallen/Najena/Guk/Unrest
  • Best long farming sessions in the game
  • Self-heal (Shaman + Lay on Hands + Mend)
Weaknesses
  • No Complete Heal (no Cleric) — emergency = Shaman big heal + LoH
  • Multi-adds need Root/Stun (no mez/charm)
  • Gear-dependent early; slow AoE clear
  • Caster mobs (necros) hit above their con

Difficulty 4 strategy

D4 = more XP, better loot, more merge duplicates. Rule: control every pull, never wipe. Slow first, tank stable, then full DPS. Bad approach = pull everything. Your trio wins by attrition, not aggression.

Suggested race and start

Suggested race — Human. Neutral faction (no kill-on-sight, easy shopping) and a Freeport start right next to Befallen, your first farm zone. The Froglok is the equally-solid alternative: Lake Rathe start, born friendly with the living Guk frogloks.
Suggested start — Paladin first, then add Shaman at creation for slow and heals from level 1, then Monk at level 10.

Stat priorities

Paladin
AC → HP → Resists → WIS
Monk
Haste → Weapons → STR → DEX → HP
Shaman
WIS → Mana → HP → Resists

🗺️ Travelling between farm zones (Norrath is huge)

From → To🥾 Overland⚡ Shortcut
Qeynos → BlackburrowNorth Qeynos → Qeynos Hills → NE corner (gnoll-head canopy)adjacent — bind Qeynos
→ Befallen (W. Commonlands)Qeynos Hills → W/N/E Karana → Highpass → Kithicor (night = death) → West Commonlands → south-wall tunnel🧙 Druid/Wizard port to Commonlands / W.Freeport — no Qeynos→Freeport boat, else all overland
Befallen → Najena (Lavastorm)West → East Commonlands → Nektulos Forest → Lavastorm → SE double doors🧙 druid/wizard port to Nektulos
→ Crushbone / Unrest (Faydwer)—overland Qeynos→Freeport, then 🚢 East Freeport → OoT → Butcherblock (or dock translocator ~21pp, instant) → Greater Faydark (Crushbone) / Dagnor's Cauldron (Unrest); or 🧙 port
→ Upper/Lower Guk (Innothule)Qeynos → S. Karana → Lake Rathetear → Rathe Mtns → Feerrott → Innothule Swamp → Guk🧙 port to Innothule/Feerrott
→ The Hole (Odus)—🚢 boat Qeynos → Erudin → Toxxulia → Paineel → descend
🚢 Boats: East Freeport ↔ Ocean of Tears ↔ Butcherblock (→ Faydwer) · Qeynos ↔ Erudin (→ Odus). There is NO Qeynos↔Freeport boat — Qeynos and Freeport are both on Antonica, you cross overland (Karanas → Commonlands). A flex Druid/Wizard slot (ports/gate) is the real shortcut. ⚡ Translocators: at the boat docks (Freeport · Butcherblock · Ocean of Tears) NPC translocators TP you directly between the boat stops for ~21pp (confirm price in game) — no waiting for the boat.

🔁 Named Farm Loops — how to farm intelligently

🔍 On the maps below: mouse wheel = zoom · drag = pan · double-click = reset.
The EverQuest way: don't sit and wait on one spawn. Clear the named in a fixed loop; by the time you circle back to the start, they've respawned. Revamped zones (Befallen/Najena/Blackburrow, and now Crushbone and Splitpaw) have guaranteed named, no placeholders → every loop pays. Refill mana while walking (Shaman Cannibalize) so there's zero downtime.
▶ Befallen route (with /loc — type /loc and match Y,X):
Entrance → Central (named skeletons = Pristine) → East corridor Thaumaturgist (-486,86) → West corridor Cmdr Windstream (-626,-92) → bottom Baron Telyx V`Zher (-1002,-80) → Priest Amiaz (-494,95) · Boondin (-814,-206) → loop. Keys (SK): (-334,183) → (-552,-54) → (-511,152). Troll SK (-372,-105).
▶ Najena route (/loc — from your Brewall pack):
Unbound Flame (Flaming Fist · L25 · respawn 5min · Stun/Root) (-26,360) → Bonecracker (250,173) → Officer Grush (91,234) → Rathyl (-154,-116) → Drelzna (L25 · 18.5min · Tentacle Whip / Bronze Greaves for Pal) (-35,-222) → The Tenderizer (L22 · Kitchen Toolbelt + fast 1H blunt) (131,342) → Najena (Clawed Knuckle Ring ~10%) (351,105). Also: Trazdon (357,217), Ekeros (-162,-50).
▶ Unrest route (with /loc):
Yard → 1st floor Lesser Blade Fiend (699,90) → 2nd floor Reclusive Ghoul Magus (700,27) → 4th floor Undead Knight (697,-76) → basement (fake-library door) Garanel (485,147).
▶ Lower Guk route (/loc — from your Brewall pack, dead side):
Frenzied Ghoul (FBSS) (1063,-1056) → Ghoul Cavalier (Dark Reaver) (580,-612) → Executioner (625,-884) → Sentinel (852,-1085) → Assassin (638,-775) · Supplier (634,-766) → Froglok King (-107,20). Ghoul Lord (Sword of Ykesha) (1558,-749) = cul-de-sac just NE of the Jail Room. Bring an evac.

Befallen loop (10-15)

Named — loot (👆 hover a pin on the map to see its name) : ① Splintered key — SK door1 · ② Thaumaturgist — top loot · ③ Priest Amiaz — wand · ④ Smoked key — SK / Barbed · ⑤ Cmdr Windstream — gear · ⑥ Boondin — Icon of the Ardent · ⑦ Baron Telyx V`Zher — Pristine Studded Tunic AC14 / Baron's Blade

Najena loop (18-25)

Named — loot (👆 hover a pin on the map to see its name) : ① Unbound Flame — Flaming Fist · ② Bonecracker — dull bone key · ③ Officer Grush — shiny metal key · ④ Rathyl — Trav. Pouch · ⑤ Drelzna — Stiletto / Tentacle Whip · ⑥ The Tenderizer — Kitchen Toolbelt · ⑦ Najena — Clawed Knuckle Ring

Lower Guk camp chain (30-50)

Named — loot (👆 hover a pin on the map to see its name) : ① Ghoul Lord — Sword of Ykesha · ② Frenzied Ghoul — FBSS · ③ Ghoul Sentinel · ④ Executioner · ⑤ Assassin · ⑥ Supplier · ⑦ Cavalier — Dark Reaver

Crushbone — rebuilt 14 Jul 2026 (levels 4-22)

The named tour, low to high: zone in from Greater Faydark into the slaver caves (4-10) — the Taskmaster drops the Brass Earring. Trainer Hill (~7-12) has the Orc Trainer and his Shiny Brass Shield, spawning with no placeholder. The castle holds the warlord, Lord Darish, Marrowbane (Grimy Black Silk Robe, a caster chest piece) and Chokehold — a level 20 rogue who drops the Master Crushbone Cell Key. Lord Darish also drops the Torn Elvish Tapestry — back slot, AC 6, CHA 4, SvM 5, every class, which is the early back-slot piece Chapter 6 says does not exist. That key is No Trade and flagged quest, so Crushbone does have locked doors — no source documents them. Then the throne room (~18-22) with Emperor Crush.
Signature quest — the Screaming Mace: take the Orc Trainer's Shiny Brass Shield, the Taskmaster's Brass Earring and Emperor Crush's Dwarven Ringmail Tunic to the Dwarven Blacksmith for a Dwarven Mace, then have the Elven Priest enchant it with the Warlord's Bracers of Battle.
Level range 4-22 and the revamp itself come from the official patch notes; the inner brackets are indicative. Named positions come from the Brewall pack — confirm exact item names in game.

Splitpaw loop (25-42) — The Lair of the Splitpaw

Loot par pastille (n° = named sur la carte) : ③ Fine Steel Scimitar, Vacra Av Svim · ④ Cap of Mending, Nisch Mas Ilkvel · ⑤ A Split Paw, Devlas Ilkvel, Fine Steel Scimitar, Fine Steel Warhammer, Split Paw Hide Mask, Tesch Val Sinisch, White Hellebore · ⑥ Gnoll Hide Tome, Channeler's Gloves · ⑦ Dagger of Dropping, Kicsh Der Pavz, Rune of the Helix, Tanned Split Paw Skin, Thick Amber Potion, Words of Descrying · ⑧ Bronze Bastard Sword, Dagger of Dropping, Ruby Pendant, Split Paw Hide Mask · ⑨ Split Paw Hide Gloves, Verishe Bracer of Dominance, Verishe Mal Greataxe · ⑩ Split Paw Hide Belt · ① Brother Gruff, ② Brother Hayle — aucun drop notable · Zixx Nenix (no /loc yet)
⚔️ Splitpaw field notes (community review) — dangers & hidden paths
  • Cleric mobs are the real danger — they stun and heal, packed into the early twisting sections and around the Executioner (the Judges). Pacify lets you skip the Judges entirely.
  • Door maze — easy to train yourself. The mid sections are cramped twisting halls full of doors and dead-ends; after each door head south or west.
  • 🔧 Lever door + trap room. Reaching the level-39 area needs a tricky door opened by a lever that is hard to target from your side — and beware a small lever room in the hall that can trap you.
  • 🌊 Hidden water tunnel. The level 42-45 area (Executioner / Ishva) is reached by swimming through a hidden water tunnel around the middle of the level-39 section.
  • Best early drop: the Nisch Mas Ilkvel shield from A Nisch Mas Mender (30) has a mana-preservation focus — a top pickup for any priest-class trio.
Map key: ● zone line/entrance · ■ lever (opens a door or the jail cells, from the Brewall map data). Field intel from a community review (Chaz & Omaha).

Estate of Unrest (14-35)

Aligned floorplan — one map per floor. Mouse wheel or buttons to zoom.

Unrest · Step 1 — ⭐ THE HAND (3rd floor, NO KEY) → Small Stone Key (magus room) · also Undead Barkeep → Scuffed Silver Key (the tower)

Unrest · Step 2 — 2nd floor: Reclusive Ghoul Magus → Polished Brass Key

Unrest · Step 3 — 4th floor: Undead Knight (→ Bloodstained Tunic + Cracked Glass Key)

Unrest · Step 4 — Basement: Garanel Rucksif (final target → Dwarven Work Boots)

Unrest · Step 5 — 1st floor: Lesser Blade Fiend (rare spawn — Gladius) + the library

Chaining rules

  • Loop length ≈ respawn. Move so a full circuit takes roughly as long as the named take to repop (~8-22 min). Too fast = you wait; too slow = you leave loot on the ground.
  • Slow → tank → burst every pull. Don't skip Slow to "save time" — a wipe costs more than a slow.
  • No downtime: Shaman Cannibalize + med while walking to the next named; pull the next as soon as mana's up.
  • Priority: hit the S named every loop (Tenderizer, Baron Telyx, Frenzied Ghoul, Ghoul Lord); A/B only if up and on the way.
  • Exact EQL respawns are mostly undocumented — time your first few loops in-game and adjust.

Chapter 3 — Dungeon Atlas

Step-by-step directions — landmark by landmark, so you know exactly where each named/key/secret is without me redrawing the map. Keep a real map (Brewall in-game, or EQ Atlas) open beside it; the /loc numbers pin each spot.
🫥 Navigate with invisibility (key skill): your Invisibility to Undead (IVU) — lvl 17 — lets you walk past undead unseen — huge for reaching a named/camp in Befallen, Najena, Unrest, Guk dead side without grinding through every mob. Normal Invisibility (vs the living) — lvl 27 — does the same vs living mobs. ⚠️ Travel only — invis breaks the moment you attack, and some mobs see through it (Lower Guk vampire bats see invis; higher-level / see-invis undead ignore IVU; casters can ignore it). Move slowly, don't bump aggro.
Befallen (map ref: EQ Atlas ↗)
  1. Entrance: you arrive at the top of a staircase; the main hall below holds ~4 skeletons all in each other's aggro range. Pull singly back up the stairs — don't wade in. This hall is your Pristine Leather/Chain + magic-weapon farm.
  2. The well = your hub (centre of floor 1). The room just WEST of the well = Skeleton Lrodd + the Dark Elf SK who drops the Splintered Wooden Key (door 1) -334,183. The pit room (female SK) = Charred Bone Key (door 2) -552,-54. The Ogre SK (also drops Barbed Armplates) = Smoked Glass Key (door 3) -511,152.
  3. Drop down the well → floors 2/3. First right after you land = Priest Amiaz -494,95. Down here: Baron Telyx, Cmdr Windstream, Thaumaturgist. Troll SK (any key + Bone Bladed Claymore) -372,-105.
  4. Danger: floor-3 necromancers cast while running and hit above con — kill them first. Never sit/loot mid-room (it widens aggro radius → swarm). Gynok Moltor (rare, Ring of Opolla) spawns by the WC zone-line -80,-45.
  5. Escape: bind at the entrance. The entry tunnel is long — if a train is in it, wait it out, don't chase.
Najena (map ref: EQ Atlas ↗)
  1. Front rooms: each packs ~4 skeletons + earth elementals + ogre guards, all aggro. Below ~17, farm only these front rooms and pull to the door.
  2. The key chain IS the route (in order): find Bonecracker (in a room with 2 dark-elf necros) → dull bone key. Kill the Ogre Captain → shiny metal key. Keep both — they're the only non-No-Rent keys. (Patch Jul 7: locked doors now auto-search your bags for keys — no need to hold a key on the cursor.)
  3. Shiny metal key opens the cell outside Rathyl; dull bone key opens Rathyl's area — you need both to reach him. Rathyl → bloodstained key + Traveller's Pouch (-25% wt).
  4. Bloodstained key → Drelzna's room. Drelzna → golden crescent key → opens Najena's room via the underwater tunnel. The Widow Mistress (Stiletto) is by the spiders beyond that underwater tunnel.
  5. The Tenderizer → Kitchen Toolbelt (haste) (also drops the fast 1H-blunt "The Tenderizer" weapon) — hit it every pass, your monk priority.
  6. Unbound Flame — fire elemental, L25, -26,360, respawn 5 min, Stun/Root → Flaming Fist (monk H2H, +20 fire). Kill it fast, it's a caster.
  7. Najena herself (deep, via the underwater tunnel) → Clawed Knuckle Ring (~10%) + Flowing Black Robe.
  8. Secret/danger: 2 pit traps on the upper level drop you into the prison. Do NOT attack Moosh in the prison — it wrecks Rivervale faction.
Estate of Unrest (map ref: EQ Atlas ↗)
  1. Yard (10-16): death beetles + tormented dead roam — fight along the back & west walls. Lesser Blade Fiend (→ Gladius) 699,90 is in the 1st-floor back room.
  2. Key chain (launch order) — 1st: the first named, the Hand (1st floor), drops the Small Stone Key → opens the magus room.
  3. 2nd: through it → Reclusive Ghoul Magus on the balcony 700,27 (→ Savant's Cap) drops the Polished Brass Key.
  4. 3rd: that key opens the bar room → the Barman / undead barkeep drops the Scuffed Silver Key, which opens the tower.
  5. 4th floor: that key → Undead Knight of Unrest 697,-76 (→ Bloodstained Tunic; Harm Touch + Fear). He also drops the Cracked Glass Key — the one that opens the basement.
  6. Garanel (basement): the Cracked Glass Key from the Undead Knight opens the way down → Garanel Rucksif 485,147 → Dwarven Work Boots + Jagged Band. ⚠️ Mortuary Fungus (drowsy) + fake-lava trap on the way. The Priest of Najena drops the Tarnished Bronze Key, which opens nothing in Unrest — it is for the Mystic Cloak quest in Najena, so do not go hunting him for a door. Per the EQL wiki these keys only drop during the classic era and are only needed then; afterwards the doors stand unlocked.
  7. Escape: Hand Room → balcony → patio roof = straight shot to the zone line. Bind at the entrance.
Lower Guk (map ref: dead ↗ / live ↗)
  1. Getting in: from Upper Guk, three entrances drop near Dead side (two need a short swim, the dry one is closest to the front entrance). The single Live-side entrance is the deepest into Upper Guk.
  2. Dead side (your undead farm): chain Frenzied Ghoul (FBSS) → Ghoul Lord (1558,-749) (Sword of Ykesha — cul-de-sac NE of the Jail Room) → King → Executioner → Assassin → Magi, then loop.
  3. Danger: vampire bats see through invis (dead side); on Live side the Huge Water Elemental sees invis and nearby frogloks assist it; caster frogloks (shaman/wizard) — kill first, they run.
  4. Escape: Dead side is far from every exit — always carry an evac. The King's Room on Live side has a back zone-exit to Upper Guk (but it runs you past the toughest mob, emergency use only).
Blackburrow ↗ · farm entrance ring (7-9); avoid pit/lower tunnels (trains). · Runnyeye ↗ · goblin loop. · Sol A ↗ Bridge→Bartender→Forge. · Sol B ↗ 40+, avoid Lord Nagafen. · Mistmoore ↗ vampires 20-35. · The Hole ↗ 40+, evac. · Fear/Hate 46+ raid-tier.

Chapter 4 — Named Database

How to find a named: each entry shows its /loc (Y,X) or its room. In game type /loc — it prints your Y, X, Z; walk until your Y,X match the named's numbers. (Najena/Guk named are room-based — follow the key chain / camp order.) EQL beta: exact respawn & drop rates for revamped named aren't documented — marked TBC. Classic timers shown where applicable.
Baron Telyx V`Zher S-Tier — Befallen (bottom) · (-1002,-80) · DE Paladin L28
Respawn 4:27 · Loot: Pristine Studded Leather Tunic (CHEST AC14 STR/AGI/DEX+2 SVPoison+5, mergeable) + The Baron's Blade (1H Slash 10/30 DEX+5 STA+5 HP+5) · Pull: single, Slow first.
The Thaumaturgist A — Befallen L3 · (-486, 86)
Level 18-19 · Respawn TBC (PH removed in EQL) · Abilities: necro DoT/DD · Loot: Blackened Wand, top loot · Pull: careful, kill fast (caster).
Commander Windstream A — Befallen L3 · (-626, -92)
Level 26 · Respawn TBC · Abilities: necro nukes · Loot: excellent gear · Pull: single.
The Tenderizer S-Tier — Najena · (131,342) · Ogre WAR L22
Loot: Kitchen Toolbelt (Haste +10%) + The Tenderizer weapon (1H Blunt 5 / delay 19 — fast, Class incl MNK) · Pull: single. Top target for your Monk. Respawn TBC.
Drelzna A — Najena · (-35,-222) · DE SK L25
Respawn 18.5 min (PH = a necro at the chair in the middle of the room) · Abilities: Harm Touch (Jul 7 patch: no longer rapid-procs) · Loot: Stiletto of the Bloodclaw, Tentacle Whip (lifetap, Pal), Bronze Greaves (AC10 legs, Pal), golden crescent key · Pull: single, interrupt.
Unbound Flame A — Najena · (-26,360) · Fire Elem L25
Respawn 5 min · HP 1080 · hits 40 · Abilities: Stun, Root · Loot: Flaming Fist (H2H 6/18 +20 fire, MNK) (Rare), Drop of Crystallized Flame (EAR) · Pull: kill fast, it's a caster.
Najena A — Najena (deep) · (351,105)
Level ~29 · Abilities: several high-level protectors · Loot: Flowing Black Robe, Black Tome · Pull: clear protectors first.
Undead Knight of Unrest S-Tier — Unrest 4th floor (697,-76)
Level 28 · Respawn ~22 min · Abilities: Harm Touch + Fear · Loot: Bloodstained Tunic (AC13), Bloodstained Mantle · Pull: be ready for Fear (cure/position).
Garanel Rucksif A — Unrest basement (485,147)
Level 35 · Abilities: guarded by the toughest room in the zone · Loot: Dwarven Work Boots, Jagged Band · Pull: clear the room first.
Frenzied Ghoul S-Tier — Lower Guk · (1063,-1056)
Abilities: hits very hard (worse with FBSS) · Loot: FBSS (haste) · Pull: Slow instantly, tank solid.
Ghoul Lord S-Tier — Lower Guk (dead side) · (1558,-749) · cul-de-sac just NE of the Jail Room
Level 47 — and never alone. Two ghoul knights stand with him (dar and bok, seen at level 40 and 43 — they vary by spawn), respawns come back fast, and roamers arrive mid-fight because the fight is long. This is not a single pull.
The geometry is the whole fight. He sits in an alcove at the end of a narrow corridor, and the ghoul knights stand against the side walls of that corridor. The moment you engage him they are behind you — there is no angle from which you face all three, and no room to back out.
They shield each other. Expect the log line "a bok ghoul knight begins to use a bok ghoul knight as a living shield" — these knights actively cover one another, so killing them is slower than their level suggests, which drags the fight out and brings the roamers.
What works: root pull → kill → Feign Death. Root the adds so they are out of the fight for a fixed time, kill your target, then drop to shed whatever is left. Do not try to split this camp with Feign Death alone (see Chapter 11) — the cul-de-sac is far too short for the aggro memory to clear.
Feign Death between every kill is not optional here. The replacement spawns while you are still standing in the room — kill one, and the next is on you before you have looted. Dropping after each kill is what turns this camp from a wipe into a farm: kill, FD, let it settle, stand up, take the next. Without it you are fighting an endless queue.
Doable from 35+ with patience and a little luck — field-tested at 37: three kills for about ten deaths. Dying costs no experience (see Chapter 11), so the only real price is the run back. In a private instance the fast respawns make it harder, not easier.
Loot: Short Sword of the Ykesha (1H Slash 8/24 · WAR PAL RNG SHD BRD ROG · proc Ykesha = interrupt cast + hate 425). The L37 is the proc requirement, not his level. A +2 version exists at 10/24. Also drops the Skull-shaped Barbute.
Ghoul Cavalier A — Lower Guk · (580,-612)
Level ~37 (SK) · Abilities: Harm Touch · Loot: Dark Reaver, Adamantite Epaulets.

Chapter 5 — Complete Loot Database (by slot)

SlotItemSourceStatsKeep untilBest class
ChestBloodstained TunicUnrest UKAC13 WIS+4 AGI+4⭐⭐⭐Pal/Sha

"Why it's good / upgrade path" is in the per-class BiS ladders (Ch 6-8). Pristine/Enchanted base pieces (Befallen) → merge to +10 fill every remaining slot early.

Chapter 6 — Trio Best in Slot (ONE character)

It's one body, one set of slots — not three characters. EQL is a solo game (that's exactly why one character carries 3 classes) → BiS = survive + self-sustain + haste, not group roles; aggro/hate stats are wasted solo (nobody to hold aggro from). Priority per slot: AC + HP first (you tank in D4) → one Haste item (biggest DPS jump) → keep some WIS (Shaman mana). Plate everywhere for AC.
Guide-defining rule: a BiS you can grab at a low level = priority #1 — it lasts for ages. Earliest priority pickups: Befallen ~12 (Barbed arms/legs, Antiqued Silver Band), Najena ~20 (Kitchen Toolbelt = haste), Upper Guk ~30 (Ghoulbane). Grab those the moment you're the right level.
SlotGet @
~lvl		BiS (early→mid)Source · zone · named · /locWhy (hybrid)
Head~22Savant's Cap (AC2 WIS+3 INT+3)Unrest · reclusive ghoul magus · (700,27)WIS; swap Small Bronze Helm (AC10) for tank pulls
Face~18Small Bronze Mask (AC5)Unrest · undeadAC
Ear ×2~15-25Opalline Earring (AC2 CHA+5) + Drop of Crystallized Flame (AC1 MANA10 INT1)Unrest barkeep · Najena Unbound Flame1 AC + a bit of mana (Shaman)
Neck~18Small Bronze Collar (AC6)Unrest · undeadAC
Shoulders~25Bloodstained Mantle (AC6 STR+3 SVMagic+10)Unrest · undead knight · (697,-76)AC + magic resist
Chest ★~25-28Bloodstained Tunic (AC13 WIS+4 AGI+4) or Pristine Studded Leather Tunic (AC14, mergeable)Unrest undead knight (697,-76) · Befallen Baron (-1002,-80)Bloodstained = WIS (Shaman); Pristine = +AC & merge
Back~17 ★Torn Elvish Tapestry (AC6 CHA+4 SvM+5)Crushbone · Lord Darish (167,91)Attunable, every class — verified in game 21 Jul 2026, no source lists it
Arms~12 ★Barbed Armplates (AC7 DEX+2)Befallen · shadowknight (mob) · (-988,-195)AC + DEX · early priority
Wrist ×2~20Small Bronze Bracers (AC7) + Ivory Bracelet (AGI+3 MANA+20)Unrest · reanimated hand (572,-15)1 AC, 1 mana
Hands~18Small Bronze Gauntlets (AC9)Unrest · undeadAC
Waist ★~20 → ~30Kitchen Toolbelt (Haste +10%) → upgrade to FBSS (Haste +21%)Najena · The Tenderizer (131,342) → Lower Guk · Frenzied GhoulFBSS = the BiS (21% > 10%, both pure haste). Wear Kitchen Toolbelt from ~20; swap to FBSS ~30 → it doubles your haste = your biggest DPS jump
Legs~12 ★Barbed Legplates (AC8 AGI+2) / Small Bronze Greaves (AC10)Befallen shadowknight (-988,-195) / UnrestAC · early priority
Feet ★~30Dwarven Work Boots (AC8 STR+3 DEX+5)Unrest · Garanel · (485,147)AC + STR/DEX
Finger ×2~12 / ~30Antiqued Silver Band (AC2) → Jagged Band (AC5 STR+3)Befallen SK / Unrest Garanel (485,147)AC · Antiqued is an early pickup
Primary ★30–37Survival (PAL, +shield): Ghoulbane (15/34, Dismiss Undead DD 140-162 vs undead) or Sword of Ykesha (8/24, fast · proc interrupt enemy casts) · DPS (Monk) — best = 2× Flaming Fist (both H2H, 6/18 ratio 0.333, one in each hand; monk H2H bonus applies to both hands). No Trade — farm both on Unbound Flame. Don't merge the pair together (merge = 2 copies → one +1 weapon, you'd lose your off-hand) — keep them separate; feed extra copies to +up each fist. Stopgap until the 2nd drops: Flaming Fist + The Tenderizer (5/19). Upgrade → Wu's Fist of Mastery (H2H, base 16/22 ratio 0.727 → mergeable, +3 = 20/22 = 0.909; MNK-only, No Trade) — reward from the Monk Test of Fists quest (steps/level — see eqlwiki). Wu's is ~3× the Flaming Fist ratio → your endgame monk fist.Ghoulbane = Upper Guk Shin Lord (-5,288) (L30, PAL only) · Ykesha = Lower Guk Ghoul Lord (1558,-749) (proc L37)Solo → Ykesha's +425 hate is wasted (no group); keep it for the interrupt vs caster mobs. Otherwise Ghoulbane's undead DD or dual Flaming Fist win.
Secondary~30Small Kite Shield (AC8)Unrest · Garanel · (485,147)shield = AC; drop for 2nd weapon on safe fights
Range/Charmlaterleave empty early—stat-stick slots — fill at endgame, not a farm priority now
Merge: Small Bronze + Pristine pieces drop in duplicates → merge each toward +10 (a merged Small Bronze set beats most of this by the mid-30s). TBC = EQL/higher-level slots I won't invent — confirm on Discord / /loc. Brewall isn't in EQL yet — use the in-game map + /loc. Note "shadowknight"/"SK" in the Source column = the MOB that drops the item, not a class requirement — Barbed Armplates, Small Bronze, Bloodstained, etc. all list PAL in their usable classes, so your Paladin wears them.
🔺 Haste cap & stacking: total haste caps at ~80% before lvl 50, 100% at 50+. Three separate categories that stack together: worn/item (your belt) + spell (Quickness) + Monk discipline (~20%). Within a category only the highest counts → wear one haste belt (FBSS 21% > Kitchen Toolbelt 10%); a 2nd haste item does nothing. So: keep FBSS AND cast Quickness (they add), don't drop one for the other, and don't waste a slot on a second haste item. FBSS 21% + Quickness + Monk disc nears the ~80% cap — anything past the cap is wasted.
⚔️ Weapon choice in the field (solo, ~28+): Default = Ghoulbane + shield in undead zones — its Dismiss Undead proc (140-162) nearly matches dual-fist DPS vs undead, and you keep the shield's AC/block (huge when you're your own healer). Switch to 2× Flaming Fist when: (a) the mob is living (Ghoulbane's proc does nothing → fists win DPS), or (b) the fight is safe — Slow landed, small pull — for max kill speed. Trigger = your Slow: not-yet-slowed / risky = Ghoulbane+shield; slowed & under control = dual fists.

Chapter 7 — Trio Strategy (ONE character, 3 class kits)

Read this first: you are one character with three class toolkits. When this guide says "Monk pulls → Shaman slows → Paladin tanks", that's you using your Monk pull, then casting your Shaman Slow, then holding aggro with Paladin — all on the same body, in sequence. There is no group. Your limit is your own global cooldowns and mana, so act in the right order, not all at once.
⚔️ Crushbone field findings (21 Jul 2026) — three tools you did not have
  • A pull tool before the Monk. The Deteriorated Ancient Faydark Longbow (orc warlord) is Paladin-usable. Until now this guide had no ranged pull below the Monk kit — now you can body-pull single orcs out of the castle rooms instead of taking the whole room.
  • A damage shield without a Magician. The Spiky Splintmail (Bloodgurgler) has a clickable Shield of Fire, required level 1. Click it before a multi-pull: every orc hitting you takes damage, which is exactly the situation where a solo trio loses time.
  • Bloodgurgler is a level 19 Enchanter with a pet. He mezzes and charms. He is the one named in Crushbone that can turn a routine pull into a death — kill the pet first, or pull him away from the other orcs.
🎛️ Build @ ~28 (solo undead farm) — stances · skills · spell bar

Stances & Invocations (Actions [L]; drain endurance/stamina)

Key rule: 1 Stance + 1 Invocation active at the same time — pick one of each for the situation.

Your STANCES (Pal/Monk/Sha): Offensive (+100% melee dmg, +25% hit) · Striker (weapon-skill hits ×3, +25% hit — if you spam kicks) · Defensive (−50% phys / −20% magic) · Mage Hunter (−50% magic / −20% phys) · Evasive (95% evade, monk) · Balanced (−10% all dmg, +endurance regen).

Your INVOCATIONS: Unyielding (HP regen ×2 + 25% resist Fear/mez/charm — huge vs the Undead Knight's Fear) · OverChannel (−150 resist → your Slow/DoTs LAND) · SpellBlade (gem-1 spell becomes a melee proc — put Stun/nuke there) · Recovery (mana regen ×2) · Divine (spending mana also heals you) · Empowering (+spell dmg & heal power).

Best combos @28 (undead): ① Offensive + Unyielding = default burst (dmg + HP regen + fear resist) · ② Mage Hunter/Defensive + OverChannel = caster rooms / when Slow won't stick · ③ Offensive + SpellBlade (gem-1 = Stun) = melee that procs spells · ④ Balanced + Recovery = mana-starved. Watch the yellow stamina bar.

Skills (level by using — keep maxed)

Defense · Dodge · Parry · Riposte · Block (shield) · Offense · Dual Wield · Hand-to-Hand + 1H Slashing (Ghoulbane) · Bind Wound. Abilities: Mend + Feign Death (Monk), Lay on Hands (Paladin).

Spell bar — 8 gems

  1. Slow (Sha) — the fight
  2. Heal (Sha)
  3. Regen / HoT (Sha) — sustain
  4. Cannibalize (Sha) — HP→mana
  5. Malaise (Sha) — lands Slow/DoT + debuff
  6. Dismiss Undead / undead nuke (Pal) — DPS vs undead
  7. Stun (Pal) — interrupt necros + burst
  8. DoT (Sha) — extra DPS (or Root for 2-3 pulls)

Cast before the fight, then swap out of the bar: SoW · STR/STA + AC buff · IVU (navigation) · Cure. Exact EQL spell names/levels — confirm in-game or eqlwiki "Stances & Invocations" / "Sample Build".

Pulling
  • Monk pulls 1 by line-of-sight; FD-split at 17+.
  • Pull to a safe corner, back to a wall.
Crowd control
  • Shaman Root extras; Paladin stun (undead); Monk FD to reset a bad pull.
Slow rotation
  • Slow the instant you engage, before DPS. Re-slow if it wears.
Healing
  • Shaman heals main; Mend on cooldown.
Emergency rotation
  • Shaman biggest heal → Lay on Hands → FD (Monk) if it's collapsing. (No Complete Heal — no Cleric.)
Mana management
  • Cannibalize between pulls; med only when needed; SoW to reposition.
Multi-pulls & boss
  • 2-3: Root extras, kill slowed one first.
  • Boss: Slow → tank stable → Monk full DPS behind → save LoH for the spike. Long fight = your win condition.
  • Named farming (D4): loop the named circuit (see Farm Atlas) — by the time you return they've repopped.

Chapter 8 — Money Making

LvlBest plat farmWhat sells
8-18CrushboneCracked Staffs, Fine Steel, cloth
10-25Befallenundead cash, bronze, rawhide
30-50Lower Guk★ FBSS / Ykesha / Runed Falchion — high value
32-36Hill Giants (Rathe/Karana)raw plat per kill
  • Vendor: sell to a merchant you have good faction with; rotate to avoid crashing their coin.
  • Quests: Gnoll Fangs (Captain Tillin), Crushbone belts.
  • Crushbone casters: Grimy Black Silk Robe (Marrowbane) is useless to this trio but sells — INT/Mana chest pieces move fast in the first weeks. Note it drops from Marrowbane, not from the Prophet as commonly listed.
  • Keep, don't sell: Pristine/Enchanted, haste, WIS gear, merge duplicates.

Chapter 9 — Raid Preparation

TargetAccess / LvlResist focusNote
Plane of Hateportal 46+Magicclass planar armor
Plane of Fearportal 46+Magicentry-level raid gear
Plane of SkykeyMagicquest accessories, islands
Nagafen (Sol B)~50Firedragon — FR gear mandatory
Vox (Permafrost)~50Colddragon — CR gear mandatory
Cazic Thule~46+Magicfear god, Feerrott
Innoruuk (Hate)50Magichate god EQL: does less melee/round than Cazic per patch
  • Min gear: +7/+10 merged set, Ghoulbane, haste, swappable resist set.
  • Consumables: food/drink, bandages, mana + heal items, resist potions, SoW.
  • Exact EQL flags/keys per plane — confirm on Discord.

Chapter 10 — Endgame

  • Best AA (early): Quick Buff, Origin, Spellblade Invocation, run speed/regen — the AA pool is shared across your 3 classes. Exact EQL AA order — confirm on Discord.
  • Final gear: full +10 set, epic 1.0 per class, planar pieces, best haste + resist set.
  • Best trio build: Paladin primary (AC/LoH/anti-undead) for survivability, or Shaman primary for sustain-lead; the Monk is always your kill slot.
  • Augments: tradeskill/quest augments in every slot; cultural armor rivals raid gear for many slots. EQL augment sources — confirm.
  • Upgrade path: merge set +10 → named dungeon drops → epic → planar → cultural → raid gear.

★ Bonus — Revamped dungeons & checklist

Revamped (guaranteed named, no placeholders): Befallen, Blackburrow, Najena, and — since the 14 Jul 2026 patch — Crushbone (4-22) and Lair of the Splitpaw (25-42). Watch patch notes / Discord for future revamps.

Completion checklist

  • Befallen — leather set merged, Barbed plate, Baron Telyx V`Zher (Pristine Studded Tunic)
  • Najena — Flaming Fist, Kitchen Toolbelt, Clawed Knuckle Ring
  • Upper Guk — Ghoulbane
  • Unrest — Bloodstained Tunic, Dwarven Work Boots (+ Small Bronze to merge)
  • Lower Guk — FBSS, Sword of Ykesha
  • The Hole — Idol of the Underking
  • Fear / Hate — raid gear

Chapter 11 — Legends systems (what classic EQ players get wrong)

EQ Legends is not classic EverQuest with new maps. Several core rules changed, and they silently invalidate habits you brought from P99 or TLP. Read this before you lock your classes, before you sell a duplicate, and before you decide a named is out of reach.
🗺️ Do this before you play: install Brewall's maps.
The map system shipped with the beta shows almost nothing in most zones. Brewall's map pack fixes it completely — detailed, labelled maps for every zone — and the download and installation instructions are all on his page:
eqmaps.info — EQ map files

Why it matters here in particular: the eight annotated dungeon maps in this guide are drawn on that same Brewall geometry. Install the pack and your in-game map matches these pages corridor for corridor — the entrance, the trap, the fake wall and the key room are exactly where this guide puts them. Without it you are navigating blind against a guide that assumes you can see.
Beta situation — the shipped map system may improve at launch.

1 · One character, three classes — and one irreversible choice

In Legends you do not roll three characters. One character carries three full class kits at once — every spell, ability, stance, invocation and gear access of all three, combined. It is not a hybrid watered down into one: it is a fusion. Your Paladin plate, your Monk strikes and your Shaman slow all live in the same body.
⚠️ How the trio opens up — and the one choice you cannot undo.
Level 1: you start by picking two classes.
Levels 1-9: you play with those two. You can still change your mind — nothing is fixed yet, so use these levels to find out what the kits actually feel like.
Level 10: the third class opens. Your trio is complete.
Level 11 and beyond: to keep levelling you must lock your three classes — and the lock is permanent, all the way to 50 and past it.
Those first ten levels are the only free trial you get. Spend them testing, not rushing.
Two limits worth knowing before you build:
Only one stance and one invocation can be active at a time, whatever your three classes offer — you pick a mode, you do not stack them.
Casters are interrupted when hit. A caster in a trio that tanks will not stand and nuke; plan around pre-cast DoTs and instant effects instead.
Why Paladin / Monk / Shaman works: the Paladin holds the aggro and wears the armour, the Monk supplies sustained melee damage, and the Shaman keeps you alive — its slow cuts enemy attack speed by half. One body, three roles, no group required. That slow is also why it is the first thing you should feed motes into (see below).

2 · Alternate Advancement — from level 1, not level 51

In classic EverQuest, AAs opened at level 51. In Legends you earn them from level 1. Open the window with V. There is no AA slider — the rate is fixed, you cannot trade levelling speed for AA points, so they simply accumulate while you play. Four tabs: General (every class), Archetype and Class (depend on your three choices — you get the combined lists of all three), and Special (unlocked by achievements).
⚠️ The real difficulty in this game is stun, not damage. Pull two or three mobs and you can be stun-locked — or chain-stunned and never get control back. It is the most common way a fight you should have won turns into a corpse run.

The answer is an AA: Steadfast Will. General tab, available at level 1, 8 ranks costing 2/2/3/3/3/3/6/12 = 34 points total, ending at a 100% chance to endure a stunning melee attack, from any angle. For a melee trio this is the best 34 points in the game. Rush it.

Field data, level 37, three Dark Ghoul Knights at once in Lower Guk: the combat log fills with "You avoid the stunning blow" — nine of them on a single screen. Without rank 8 every one of those lines is a stun, and three knights stunning in rotation is the chain-stun that ends the fight for you.
Its caster-side counterpart is Persistent Casting (Archetype, 6 ranks): 11% per rank to keep your concentration and finish a cast when stunned. Steadfast Will protects your body, Persistent Casting protects your Shaman.

The one AA that fits this route perfectly

Slay Undead — Paladin class AA, 3 ranks at 3/6/9 (18 points): your melee attacks gain a chance to deal 445% / 850% / 1250% damage against undead and vampiric targets. Look at where this guide sends you: Befallen, Unrest, Lower Guk dead side — undead from level 10 to 40. There is no other 18-point purchase that multiplies your damage across your entire route like this one.
Free AAs — take them the moment they appear. Several cost 0 points: Lay on Hands (Paladin, 10 ranks, heals 6251 scaling with level), Unbound Cascade (Shaman, +10/20/30% critical DoT damage, from level 12), Origin and Gather Party (General — a free teleport home and a party summon). No reason to delay any of them.
Suggested order for Paladin / Monk / Shaman:
1 · every 0-cost AA available
2 · Steadfast Will to rank 8 — stop dying to stun-locks
3 · Slay Undead to rank 3 — your route is undead
4 · survival passives: Combat Agility, Combat Stability, Natural Durability
5 · Cannibalization (Shaman, level 40) — health into mana, the sustain this trio runs on
6 · Ambidexterity (+32% dual wield) if you fight with two weapons
Also worth a look once comfortable: Packrat (weight reduction — you will be carrying duplicates for merging), Mnemonic Retention (extra spell slots) and Improved Mend (Monk).
Full list of every AA and rank: EQL Wiki — Alternate Advancement. Several ranks are still marked unknown there.

3 · Item merging & Motes of Potential

Every piece of gear starts at Tier 0 and can be raised to Tier 10. You raise it by merging into it either another copy of the same item or a Mote of Potential. Each thing you merge adds item experience; when the bar fills, the item gains a tier.
The rule that changes your looting: stats rise continuously, not only at tier breaks. Partial progress already pays. So feed your gear whatever you have, whenever you have it — and never vendor a duplicate of something you wear. A duplicate that already dropped as +1 or better is worth several plain ones.
TierExp to nextTotal expCumulative stat bonus
010+0%
121+10%
243+20%
387+30%
41615+40%
53231+50%
66463+60%
7128127+70%
8256255+80%
9512511+90%
10—1023+100%
Read the last row again. A Tier 10 item costs 1023 exp. Merged gear gives exp equal to its own tier value (a Tier 0 copy = 1, Tier 1 = 2, Tier 2 = 4 … Tier 9 = 512), so Tier 10 is a long-term project for one chosen item, not something you do to your whole set. Pick your weapon first.
💡 The strategy the developers showed themselves: build a +10 out of a newbie-zone drop.
In the official EQ Legends Insights episode on item merging, the executive producer does not demonstrate the system on a raid item. He farms dervish cutthroats in the North Desert of Ro — a newbie zone — for a plain Cutthroat Insignia Ring: armour class 1, dexterity 2. He merges copies of it up to +10, and the finished ring gives armour class 11 and dexterity 12. His own words: easily farmed from a low-level zone.

Think about what that means for your route. The limit on a Tier 10 item is not rarity, it is how many copies you can farm. An item that drops constantly in an easy zone can end up stronger than a rare drop you will see three times in your whole climb — and you can farm it safely, at speed, at a difficulty you dominate. Pick one slot, pick an item that drops in volume, and grind it while you level.
Two details from the same demo worth keeping: at Difficulty 4 the ring dropped at +4 or better straight away, and merging a +1 copy added 2 points to the bar — because that +1 was itself made from two +0 rings. Merged gear carries its own history forward, which is exactly the table above.

Motes — and why they stop working

Motes add a fixed amount of exp, but each one has a maximum tier it can feed. Past that tier it is useless on that item. High-tier gear can only be pushed further with duplicate gear.
MoteMax tier it can feedExp given
Mote of Infinitesimal Potential01
Mote of Minor Potential11
Mote of Lesser Potential22
Mote of Potential34
Mote of Major Potential45
Mote of Greater Potential56
Mote of Superior Potential67
Mote of Grand Potential78
Mote of Ascendant Potential89
Mote of Infinite Potential910
Where motes come from: mostly named, and infrequently from trash. Higher zone difficulty raises the minimum mote tier that can drop, and killing yellow/red cons raises the drop chance. Your own level caps it too — roughly one mote tier per 5 levels (lvl 15 → Tier 3, lvl 35 → Tier 7, lvl 50 → Tier 10). Motes themselves can be upgraded.
Practical, for this trio's farm loops: the named you already kill for gear are also your mote supply. That is another reason to run the loop rather than grind trash — and a reason to run it at the highest difficulty you survive.

Levelling does not give you your spells — buy them

⚠️ Auto-scribing is a starter kit, not your spell book. New characters are auto-scribed their critical class spells up to level 20 — and that is all. Past 20 nothing is granted, and even below it the word critical is doing a lot of work: plenty of strong spells are simply never handed to you.

You have to go and buy them from vendors. Field data: at level 38, a Paladin/Monk/Shaman player bought 32 spells in one trip — thirty-two spells that levelling had never given him, several of them major. If you have never visited a spell vendor, assume you are missing a large part of your kit right now.

The game will show you what you are missing. You can browse every spell your classes can use, including the ones you have not scribed. Do that every few levels, compare it with your book, and buy the gaps — especially after 20, where nothing arrives on its own.

Spell upgrades — the trio's real power spike

Spells upgrade too, and only with motes. Open the spell's info window (Alt + Left Click, or hold Right Click on the icon in your spell bar, spellbook or the Actions window), drop a mote into the Place Mote slot, then press Upgrade. An upgraded spell gains a Roman numeral suffix — Spell II is Tier 2.
The cost curve looks identical to gear: a Tier 2 spell shows 1 / 4, the same 4 exp the gear table demands at Tier 2. Assume the same doubling — and draw the same conclusion.
Do not spread your motes. Because each tier costs double the last, feeding one mote each into twenty spells buys you almost nothing, while pouring the same motes into two spells changes fights. Concentrate.
Priority for Paladin / Monk / Shaman, in order:
1 · Your Shaman slow. Slow is what keeps this trio alive — it does more for your survival than any armour upgrade at the same cost. Tagar's Insects (SHM 27) is the one to feed while levelling.
2 · Your main DoT. Sustained damage you cast once and stop paying for — it scales across every pull of a farm loop.
3 · Buffs and debuffs, once the first two are comfortable.
Paladin heals and Monk abilities come after: the trio's bottleneck is mitigation and time-to-kill, and slow addresses both.
Still unconfirmed: whether motes carry the same per-tier cap on spells as they do on gear, and exactly which statistics each spell tier raises. Verify before betting a rare mote on a high-tier spell.

4 · Difficulty gives you merged loot for free

Raising zone difficulty does not only raise the danger: instead of the base item, mobs can drop already-merged versions, +1 to +4, with better stats and sometimes extra bonuses. This is why this guide targets Difficulty 4 — you skip several merges on every drop. It also means a lower-difficulty player misses nothing permanently; it simply takes them longer.
Difficulty also changes behaviour, not just numbers: NPCs start to multiclass, aggro radius grows, and they get more aggressive. Plan pulls accordingly.

5 · Death is cheap — push harder than your instincts say

Dying costs no experience. You respawn at your bind point, or at the zone safe point if your party is out of combat. The out-of-combat timer is 12 seconds.
If you came from classic EverQuest, this is the single habit worth unlearning. A named that kills you three times out of four is still worth attempting — the only cost is the run back. Corpse-recovery caution belongs to another game.

6 · Private instances — the farm loop tool nobody mentions

Zone difficulty works like instancing. If a difficulty gets crowded the server spins up another copy, and you can also open a private instance of a zone — with the option to choose respawns or no respawns. For a named loop this removes competition entirely and lets you control the pace.
How to open one, step by step:
1 · click the Difficulty button in the icon panel at the bottom right of the screen.
2 · choose Create Instance in the small menu that appears (it also shows your remaining charges).
3 · in the Select Instance Options window, set three things: Difficulty (0 Normal and up), Type — Solo or Multiplayer — and Respawning or Non-Respawning.
4 · press Create.
⚠️ Instances cost a charge, and you only get a couple. The options window shows Current Charges and a Next Charge countdown — roughly an hour to regenerate one. So this is not something you re-roll until the layout suits you: decide your difficulty, your type and your respawn setting before you spend one. Getting it wrong costs you an hour, not a click.
A private instance is not automatically easier. It removes competition for loot, and that is worth a lot — but it has a price. With respawns on they come back fast, and with nobody else clearing the zone every single one of them is yours. On a long named fight the adds pile up with no one to thin them. Turn respawns off when you are going for a specific kill, leave them on when you want the loop and the mote income.
⚠️ Under-levelled in your own instance is a trap. Legends is still EverQuest underneath. You can kill things ten levels above you — but three things break at that gap, and they break together:

Resists climb steeply. Your slow, DoTs and debuffs still land — but far less often. For this trio that is the dangerous one: the Shaman slow is the survival plan, and a resisted slow means you are tanking at full attack speed.
Feign Death fails far more often. Field data — Feign Death at max skill for level 37, against a level 49 mob: four failures in a row, then death.
Split pulling rarely works. One mob staying behind while the others walk off does happen, but it is the exception, not the plan your loop can rely on.

Being able to kill a mob and being able to fight it on your terms are two different things. Level is what buys you the second one — no amount of gear replaces it.
The trap that kills you after the fight is already won: aggro memory outlives the walk home.
You Feign Death, the adds turn round and head back to their spawn point. You watch them arrive, you see only one left near you, and you stand up. They come straight back. Reaching their spawn point does not mean they forgot you — the aggro memory is still holding your name.
Legends does tell you when that memory actually clears: a message along the lines of "all monsters have forgiven your location" (exact wording to be confirmed). That message is the signal — not the sight of them walking away.
And this is precisely why a small spawn room is the worst case. In the Ghoul Lord's cul-de-sac they are back on their spot in seconds, long before the memory resets, so the visual cue lies to you every time. In a tight room you simply cannot afford to wait it out — either you take the whole camp, or you do not go in.
Root pull beats Feign Death pull. It is not close.
Root pull works because it does something FD never does: it takes one mob out of the fight for a fixed time. Root the second one, kill your target before the root breaks, and you have won a clean single fight — even at a level gap.
FD pull does not work at all, and the reason is worth knowing before you waste an evening on it. The classic trick is to tag a mob with a DoT so it stays yours while you lie down — and you can see it holding, the aggro sits bright red on that mob. Then, at some point under Feign Death, every mob turns for home, and here is the killer: the DoT falls off the moment the mob decides you are dead and heads back to its spawn point. The very thing that was supposed to keep it tagged disappears with the pull. You end up with nothing separated and everything reset.
So at a wide level gap — +12 is where it becomes obvious — there is no split pull. Whether that is deliberate design against over-levelled pulling or simply how the aggro system behaves is an open question, but the practical answer is the same: root, or take the whole camp.
Do not misread this: Feign Death is still your best survival button. Everything below is about using it to split a pull — that is the part that has become unreliable. As a way to get out of a fight alive it remains the single most effective tool this trio has: you drop, the fight ends, you stand up and walk away. Two different jobs, two different verdicts. Keep it, train it, use it to survive — just do not build your pulls on it.
If you are going to rely on Feign Death, be the Monk. The two versions are not equivalent. Monk FD is a skill: instant, no mana, no cast time, and its success rate improves as the skill levels. Shadow Knight FD is a spell — it has a cast time and a mana cost, and being a spell it can be interrupted or stunned out of you, which is exactly what happens in the pulls where you need it most. For this trio that is one more argument for the Monk slot.
Community reports on r/EQLegends split down the middle on whether Feign Death still works, and the disagreement is explained by where each player is pulling.
What "double FD" actually is: distance, not a trick.
Feign Death splitting only works if the mobs have a long way to walk home. Drag them far from their spawn point first, then drop — the return trip is now long enough for the aggro-reset message to arrive before they get back. That is the whole technique. Players who report clean splits are pulling in open zones like Splitpaw, where that distance exists.
Which is exactly why it fails on a tight camp. In a small room or a cul-de-sac like the Ghoul Lord's, there is no distance to buy: they are home in seconds, the message never comes in time, and you stand up into a full camp. Add respawns on top and the room refills while you are still lying down. No amount of skill fixes a geometry problem.
And a second reason, specific to your difficulty: at D3 and D4 nearly every mob is a caster. A caster does not need to walk over to you — which makes Feign Death unreliable for a reason that has nothing to do with distance. Players farming D4 report the same thing. So on the difficulty this guide targets, plan for root and for taking the camp; treat a successful FD split as a bonus, never as the plan.

7 · Smaller rules that still bite

Planes: minimum level 46 to enter — not 45.
Personal loot: every player has their own loot pool per mob. Nobody competes with you for a drop.
Attunable: once attuned to your character an item becomes No Trade. Attune late if you might want to trade or stockpile duplicates for merging.
Meditation: sitting does nothing — you are always meditating.
Race restrictions have been removed from many items so you can use more of your loot.
Auto Loot, Auto Merge, Auto Store and Auto Sell exist in the loot settings — set Auto Merge up early.
Destroying an item pays the baseline vendor value instantly, but a vendor pays more if your Charisma is decent.
Auto-scribing gives you your critical class spells up to level 20.
Sources: EQL Wiki — Item Upgrade System and EQL Wiki — New Player Guide and EQL Wiki — Spell Upgrading, the official EQ Legends Insights — Item Merging demo, cross-checked in game. Tables reproduced with attribution; the practical reading is this guide's own. Numbers were still marked as beta-era at time of writing — verify anything that looks off and tell me.
Sourcing: map geometry = your Brewall pack (LOCEVERQUEST) · named/loc/stats = EQL Wiki + Project 1999 · zone maps = EQ Atlas · meta/patch = EQ Legends Discord. EQL beta — verify /loc & loot in game. Maps: mouse wheel / +− buttons / drag to pan / double-click reset.
